zh.lifeSummary: 當Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg於1198年出生在瑞士福包格時，他的父親Rudolf II von Habsburg28歲，母親Agnes von Staufen36歲。他和Gertraud von Regensberg至少有5個兒子。他去世於1249年7月6日享年51歲，埋葬在瑞士阿高維廷根。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg于1198年出生在瑞士福包格时，他的父亲Rudolf II von Habsburg28岁，母亲Agnes von Staufen36岁。他和Gertraud von Regensberg至少有5个儿子。他去世于1249年7月6日享年51岁，埋葬在瑞士阿高維廷根。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

Chinese: variant of   Wong  .

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Rudolf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Latinized version, Rudolphus, of the Germanic name Hrōdwulf ( see Rolf ). It was introduced to the English-speaking world from Germany in the 19th century. Rudolf was a hereditary name among the Habsburgs, the Holy Roman Emperors and rulers of Austria, from the Emperor Rudolf I ( 1218–91 ) to the Archduke Rudolf , Crown Prince of Austria-Hungary, who died in mysterious circumstances at his country house at Meyerling in 1889 . Rudolf Rassendyll was the central character of Anthony Hope's adventure stories The Prisoner of Zenda ( 1894 ) and Rupert of Hentzau ( 1898 ), in which he is an English gentleman who bears a great physical resemblance to the King of Ruritania, to whom he is distantly related. He successfully impersonates the king for reasons of state. In the early 20th century the popularity of this name was further enhanced by the American silent-film actor Rudolph Valentino ( 1895–1926 ), born in Italy as Rodolfo di Valentina d'Antonguolla . It has not been used much in recent times, perhaps being inextricably associated with ‘the red-nosed reindeer’ of the Christmas song.

variants.name: Rodolf
meanings: Variant of   Rudolf .

variants.name: Rudolph
meanings: From a Latinized version, Rudolphus, of the Germanic name Hrōdwulf ( see Rolf ). It was introduced to the English-speaking world from Germany in the 19th century. Rudolf was a hereditary name among the Habsburgs, the Holy Roman Emperors and rulers of Austria, from the Emperor Rudolf I ( 1218–91 ) to the Archduke Rudolf , Crown Prince of Austria-Hungary, who died in mysterious circumstances at his country house at Meyerling in 1889 . Rudolf Rassendyll was the central character of Anthony Hope's adventure stories The Prisoner of Zenda ( 1894 ) and Rupert of Hentzau ( 1898 ), in which he is an English gentleman who bears a great physical resemblance to the King of Ruritania, to whom he is distantly related. He successfully impersonates the king for reasons of state. In the early 20th century the popularity of this name was further enhanced by the American silent-film actor Rudolph Valentino ( 1895–1926 ), born in Italy as Rodolfo di Valentina d'Antonguolla . It has not been used much in recent times, perhaps being inextricably associated with ‘the red-nosed reindeer’ of the Christmas song.

nameMeanings.name: III.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: When Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg was born in 1198, in Fribourg, Switzerland, his father, Rudolf II von Habsburg, was 28 and his mother, Agnes von Staufen, was 36. He had at least 5 sons with Gertraud von Regensberg. He died on 6 July 1249, at the age of 51, and was buried in Wettingen, Aargau, Switzerland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg est né en 1198, à Fribourg, Suisse, son père, Rudolf II von Habsburg, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Agnes von Staufen, avait 36 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils avec Gertraud von Regensberg. Il est décédé le 6 juillet 1249, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterré à Wettingen, Argovie, Suisse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg 1198 in Freiburg, Schweiz geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rudolf II von Habsburg 28 und seine Mutter Agnes von Staufen 36. Er hatte mit Gertraud von Regensberg mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb am 6. Juli 1249 im Alter von 51 Jahren und wurde in Wettingen, Aargau, Schweiz begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg è nato il nel 1198 nel Friburgo, Svizzera, il suo padre, Rudolf II von Habsburg, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Agnes von Staufen, aveva 36 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli con Gertraud von Regensberg. Morì il 6 luglio 1249, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolto a Wettingen, Argovia, Svizzera.

ja.lifeSummary: Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburgが1198年にスイスフレイブルグで生まれた時，父Rudolf II von Habsburgは28歳，母Agnes von Staufenは36歳であった。彼はGertraud von Regensbergとの間に少なくとも5人の息子をもうけた。彼は1249年7月6日に51歳で死去し，スイスアールガウウエッティンジェンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg(이)가 1198년 스위스 프레이버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rudolf II von Habsburg(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes von Staufen(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Gertraud von Regensberg(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1249년 7월 6일 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 스위스 아아르가 베틴겐에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg nasceu em 1198, em Freiburg, Suiça, seu pai, Rudolf II von Habsburg, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Agnes von Staufen, tinha 36 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos com Gertraud von Regensberg. Ele faleceu em 6 de julho de 1249, com 51 anos, e foi sepultado em Wettingen, Aargau, Suiça.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg родился в 1198 г. в местности фрибург, Швейцария, его отцу по имени Rudolf II von Habsburg было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes von Staufen, 36. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей от Gertraud von Regensberg. Он умер 6 июля 1249 г. в возрасте 51 год и был похоронен в местности веттинген, ааргау, Швейцария.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rudolf III. von Habsburg, der Schweigsame, I. Graf von Habsburg-Laufenburg nació en 1198, en Freiburg, Suiza, su papá, Rudolf II von Habsburg, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Agnes von Staufen, tenía 36 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos con Gertraud von Regensberg. Murió el 6 de julio de 1249, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultado en Wettingen, Aargau, Suiza.
